title: Food Vouchers - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Food > Food Vouchers 5 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Older Adults Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO SNAP AND FOOD ACCESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The 211info SNAP and Food Access program provides specialized information, referral, advocacy, follow-up services, and extra guidance to individuals and family members who would like to access the SNAP program, have a question or issue with their current SNAP case, and/or need extra guidance accessing food resources. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details INFORMATIONAL RECORD DUAL HEALTH CREDIT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF OTC plus card or credit help pay for over-the-counter (OTC) healthcare products, healthy foods and household utilities. Covered items and service may include cold care, oral care, pain relief, first aid, incontinence products, grocery items like meat and produce, and utility payments such as electricity, gas, water, telephone, and internet. OTC plus card or credit plan amounts, availability, and items/services covered are dependent on applicant's Coordinated Care Organization (CCO). Confidential Address Eligibility: Must be dual enrolled in Medicare and Medicaid (Oregon Health Plan) Hours: Varies by applicant's CCO More Details NOWIA UNETE CANNABIS WORKER RESILIENCE PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with filing claims for unpaid wages, legal assistance, mental health supports, temporary lodging, food boxes and vouchers, clothing, and relocation assistance. 607 W Main Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Individuals who are employed in the illegal marijuana industry Hours: Monday-Friday noon-3pm and 4pm-7pm Email (541) 840-9669 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION SENIOR FARM DIRECT NUTRITION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides eligible seniors with vouchers that can be used at participating farm stands and markets to purchase locally grown fresh fruits and vegetables. Vouchers can be used from June 1 to November 30. 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: To be eligible for the program, seniors must meet ALL of the following criteria: -Be at least 62 years old by April 1 of the current year -Be receiving either SNAP benefits and/or Medicaid on April 1 of the current year -Have income below 138% of the Federal Poverty Level in the current year -Be living in a situation where food is not provided for them Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (866) 299-3562 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION WIC FARM DIRECT NUTRITION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vouchers that can be used at participating farm stands and markets to purchase locally grown fresh fruits and vegetables. Vouchers can be used from June 1 to November 30. 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: WIC participants Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK SERENITY CLUB COMMUNITY RESOURCE ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers financial assistance in the form of gas or grocery gift cards. Limited gift cards are available on a first-come, first-served basis. Monthly bus passes for The Wave Tillamook may be available for people without other forms of transportation. 5012 3rd Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Must be participating in 12-step recovery Hours: 2nd Tuesday of the month Email (503) 842-1115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NOWIA UNETE CANNABIS WORKER RESILIENCE PROJECT OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION SENIOR FARM DIRECT NUTRITION PROGRAM TILLAMOOK SERENITY CLUB COMMUNITY RESOURCE ASSISTANCE
